title: Living in Boulder 7-24-2017: 46.3M 4:24 5,545ft. Up Sunshine, down through Jamestown.

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about a 46.3-mile bike ride in Boulder, going up Sunshine Canyon through Gold Hill to Ward and descending through Jamestown Canyon at nearly 50 mph. He covers ride stats (148 avg watts, 5,545 ft elevation), knee pain, his surprisingly low appetite despite heavy exertion, and plans to keep riding hard until injury forces a break.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: thrilling but dangerous — road edge drop-offs demand constant alertness
eats almost nothing despite heavy riding; attributes energy to burning body fat
ride mountains hard every day until the knee gives out, then switch to swimming or running
completely one-track-minded on riding right now, and thinks that makes for more interesting content

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today I rode my bike up Sunshine Canyon through Gold Hilltown and then down to Ward.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got some water at the Fountain of Ward.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Tomorrow I'm going to work to figure out how to get all my stuff working.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rode on a pizza pick back down from James Canyon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A guy in a truck told me to turn around, so I went a mile farther where there was a sign for James Town.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Biking down James Canyon is really fast, almost 50 miles an hour.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm not used to zooming really fast while being really high.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The mountains and cars can be dangerous, especially where the asphalt breaks off into holes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Biking requires being really alert, especially on dirt roads.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I averaged around 200 watts for 10 minutes to an hour during my ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My average watts for the 46-mile ride was 148.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I love climbing and descending on my bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't tested the maximum braking power because I'm afraid of breaking the 4x.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I weigh 200 pounds, so I'm cautious with braking.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to test braking at 50 miles per hour to see how quickly I can stop.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got a veggie burger and fries in James Town from a lady named Dolores.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There's a ghost town just above James Town called something I can't recall.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can ride my bike on dirt roads pretty well, but I have to be careful with normal road tires.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My appetite is low; yesterday I only ate a little corn and rice.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I brought five power bars but didn't eat any today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today I had a 7-layer burrito and a bean burrito from Taco Bell, plus a veggie burger and fries.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm burning fat for energy since I'm not eating much.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to my mom's basement to look for an extra scale to update my weight on Strava.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need a new bike bag because the current one hits my knee.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got an energy drink today but might switch to recovery drinks with protein powder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to ride mountains until I can't anymore, then recover.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My knee was painful today, especially going up Sunshine Canyon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The knee pain drains energy but isn't sharp, so I can keep riding.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll ride until my knee gives me sharp pain, then take a week off.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to get fit so I can challenge others and ride indoors more easily.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't been working on my website because I'm too tired.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm treating this time like a vacation to make things fun.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I get really fit, it'll be easier to work on my projects while riding indoors.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Tomorrow I might try a new climb or explore more.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to organize better; it took me forever to leave the house today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had to go back home twice because I forgot things.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to be more efficient and focused like a fish in a TV show.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to adjust my bike's shifting and fix my flats.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to figure out how to set up my laptop for monitoring my rides.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm currently one-track-minded about biking and not diversifying my projects.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I hope my microphone is working this time; it didn't work last time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to practice not talking so much in my videos.
